meaning: A passage between rows of shelves in a supermarket or store.
definition: A passage between rows of shelves in a supermarket or store.
example: You can find the cereal in aisle four, next to the baking supplies.

meaning: An order for a product that cannot be delivered now because it is out of stock.
definition: An order for a product that cannot be delivered now because it is out of stock.
example: My book is on backorder, so it will take two weeks to arrive.

meaning: A small shop that sells fashionable clothes, shoes, or jewelry.
definition: A small shop that sells fashionable clothes, shoes, or jewelry.
example: She bought a unique handmade dress from a local boutique.

meaning: A well-known name given to a product by the company that makes it.
definition: A well-known name given to a product by the company that makes it.
example: Brand name medicines are usually more expensive than generic ones.

meaning: Purchasing large quantities of a product at one time, usually to save money.
definition: Purchasing large quantities of a product at one time, usually to save money.
example: Bulk buying toilet paper and paper towels is much cheaper in the long run.

meaning: A basket on wheels used for carrying goods while shopping in a store.
definition: A basket on wheels used for carrying goods while shopping in a store.
example: She filled her cart with fresh vegetables and fruits.

meaning: A book or online directory showing a list of items for sale with their descriptions and prices.
definition: A book or online directory showing a list of items for sale with their descriptions and prices.
example: I flipped through the holiday catalog to find gift ideas for my family.

meaning: A small shop that is open for long hours and sells everyday items like snacks, drinks, and newspapers.
definition: A small shop that is open for long hours and sells everyday items like snacks, drinks, and newspapers.
example: He ran down to the corner convenience store to buy a carton of milk.

meaning: The assistance and advice provided by a company to those who buy or use its products.
definition: The assistance and advice provided by a company to those who buy or use its products.
example: If your new laptop does not work, call customer service for help.

meaning: Having a fault or flaw; not working properly.
definition: Having a fault or flaw; not working properly.
example: The company recalled the defective toys due to safety concerns.

meaning: A large shop divided into several sections, each selling a different type of goods.
definition: A large shop divided into several sections, each selling a different type of goods.
example: You can buy clothes, cosmetics, and kitchenware all in one department store.

meaning: To return a product to a store and get a different one instead.
definition: To return a product to a store and get a different one instead.
example: The shirt was too small, so I went to the store to exchange it for a medium.

meaning: A small room in a clothing store where you can try on clothes to see if they fit.
definition: A small room in a clothing store where you can try on clothes to see if they fit.
example: She took three dresses into the fitting room to try them on.

meaning: Buying things suddenly without planning or thinking about whether you really need them.
definition: Buying things suddenly without planning or thinking about whether you really need them.
example: Placing candy near the cash register encourages impulse buying.

meaning: Available to buy immediately in the store or online.
definition: Available to buy immediately in the store or online.
example: The bookstore had only one copy of the novel in stock.

meaning: A system of paying a deposit to secure an item for later purchase, paying the balance in installments.
definition: A system of paying a deposit to secure an item for later purchase, paying the balance in installments.
example: She put the winter coat on layaway and paid ten dollars every week until it was paid off.

meaning: A person or company involved in wholesale trade or retail business.
definition: A person or company involved in wholesale trade or retail business.
example: Online merchants must ensure their websites are secure for credit card payments.

meaning: Temporarily unavailable because all items have been sold.
definition: Temporarily unavailable because all items have been sold.
example: We are out of stock of that particular phone model, but we can order it for you.

meaning: A label attached to an item showing how much it costs.
definition: A label attached to an item showing how much it costs.
example: I liked the jacket, but when I looked at the price tag, I realized it was too expensive.

meaning: A promise given to a customer allowing them to buy an out-of-stock sale item at the sale price later.
definition: A promise given to a customer allowing them to buy an out-of-stock sale item at the sale price later.
example: The store was out of the discounted shampoo, so they gave me a rain check.

meaning: A person or business that sells goods directly to the public in small quantities.
definition: A person or business that sells goods directly to the public in small quantities.
example: Large retailers often offer lower prices than small independent shops.

meaning: A short period of time that you spend buying a lot of things.
definition: A short period of time that you spend buying a lot of things.
example: After winning the lottery, she went on a major shopping spree.

meaning: Money given by a store to a customer instead of a cash refund, which can only be spent at that store.
definition: Money given by a store to a customer instead of a cash refund, which can only be spent at that store.
example: Since I didn't have the receipt, they gave me store credit instead of cash.

meaning: A person or company offering something for sale, especially on the street or at a market.
definition: A person or company offering something for sale, especially on the street or at a market.
example: The street food vendor sells delicious tacos for only two dollars.

meaning: A written guarantee promising to repair or replace a product if it breaks within a specific period.
definition: A written guarantee promising to repair or replace a product if it breaks within a specific period.
example: The television comes with a two-year warranty that covers all repairs.

meaning: A person or company that sells goods in large quantities, usually to retailers.
definition: A person or company that sells goods in large quantities, usually to retailers.
example: The store buys its stock from a wholesaler at a lower price.

meaning: Looking at goods displayed in shop windows without intending to buy anything.
definition: Looking at goods displayed in shop windows without intending to buy anything.
example: We had no money to spend, so we just spent the afternoon window shopping.
